channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Justice Department publishes some missing Epstein files related to Trump
item.description: The Justice Department has published additional Epstein files related to allegations that President Trump sexually abused a minor after an NPR investigation found dozens of pages were withheld.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department has published additional Epstein files related to allegations that President Trump sexually abused a minor after an NPR investigation found dozens of pages were withheld.

(Image credit: Department of Justice and Getty Images/Collage by Danielle A. Scruggs/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Pregnant women in ERs took less Tylenol after Trump autism warning
item.description: A study in The Lancet finds that pregnant women in emergency rooms used less Tylenol after President Trump said it could raise their babies' risk of autism. Scientists say there is no proven link.
item.content:encoded:
A study in The Lancet finds that pregnant women in emergency rooms used less Tylenol after President Trump said it could raise their babies' risk of autism. Scientists say there is no proven link.

(Image credit: Francis Chung)
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: What you need to know about Sen. Markwayne Mullin, Trump's new pick to lead DHS
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., is his pick to replace Kristi Noem as the head of the Department of Homeland Security.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump announced Thursday that Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., is his pick to replace Kristi Noem as the head of the Department of Homeland Security.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Travel industry pushes Congress to end DHS shutdown and pay federal security workers
item.description: With the busy spring break travel season looming, travel and aviation industry leaders urged Congress to end the stalemate over DHS funding before workers at TSA and ports miss a full paycheck.
item.content:encoded:
With the busy spring break travel season looming, travel and aviation industry leaders urged Congress to end the stalemate over DHS funding before workers at TSA and ports miss a full paycheck.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Trump fires Kristi Noem as DHS chief, names Sen. Markwayne Mullin to replace her
item.description: President Trump has fired his homeland security secretary, Kristi Noem, and said Markwayne Mullin, a senator from Oklahoma, would replace her.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has fired his homeland security secretary, Kristi Noem, and said Markwayne Mullin, a senator from Oklahoma, would replace her.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Can a digital tablet cut back a country's overuse of antibiotics?
item.description: Overprescribing antibiotics breeds antibiotic resistance. A new tool aims to lower a notably high rate of such prescriptions in Rwanda.
item.content:encoded:
Overprescribing antibiotics breeds antibiotic resistance. A new tool aims to lower a notably high rate of such prescriptions in Rwanda.

(Image credit: Magali Rochat)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: They were led off course in a big race. But a fix is more complicated than prize money
item.description: Top finishers in the Atlanta half marathon are calling for U.S. track officials to ensure that Jess McClain and two other athletes aren't excluded from the world championships because of an error.
item.content:encoded:
Top finishers in the Atlanta half marathon are calling for U.S. track officials to ensure that Jess McClain and two other athletes aren't excluded from the world championships because of an error.

(Image credit: Matthew Demarko)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: No matter what happens at the Oscars, Delroy Lindo embraces 'the joy of this moment'
item.description: Lindo is nominated for best supporting actor for his role in Sinners. At the BAFTA awards on Sunday, Lindo was presenting when a man with Tourette syndrome in the audience yelled out a racial slur.
item.content:encoded:
Lindo is nominated for best supporting actor for his role in Sinners. At the BAFTA awards on Sunday, Lindo was presenting when a man with Tourette syndrome in the audience yelled out a racial slur.

(Image credit: Rebecca Cabage)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Between Megan Moroney and Ella Langley, country women rule the charts
item.description: It's a big week for women in country music — and, it turns out, for women whose songs are favored by women in figure skating.
item.content:encoded:
It's a big week for women in country music — and, it turns out, for women whose songs are favored by women in figure skating.

(Image credit: Photo by: Todd Owyoung/NBC via Getty Images; Photo by Jason Kempin/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson

item.title: A Jan. 6 rioter pardoned by Trump was sentenced to life in prison for child sex abuse
item.description: Since receiving presidential pardons, dozens of former Capitol rioters have gotten into more legal trouble. In Florida, Andrew Paul Johnson was sentenced to life in prison for child sex abuse.
item.content:encoded: Since receiving presidential pardons, dozens of former Capitol rioters have gotten into more legal trouble. In Florida, Andrew Paul Johnson was sentenced to life in prison for child sex abuse.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach
